entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: A cereal crop studied for grain yield and response to tillage systems

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of maize grain produced per unit area

entities.name: SOIL ORGANIC CARBON
entities.description: A measure of carbon stored in the soil, critical for fertility and sustainability

entities.name: CONSERVATION AGRICULTURE
entities.description: A farming method focusing on minimum soil disturbance, permanent soil cover, and crop rotations

entities.name: CONVENTIONAL TILLAGE
entities.description: A traditional farming method involving intensive soil cultivation

entities.name: MANURE APPLICATION
entities.description: The practice of adding animal manure to soil to improve fertility

entities.name: CROP RESIDUE RETENTION
entities.description: The practice of leaving crop residues on the soil surface

entities.name: REDUCED TILLAGE
entities.description: A method of minimizing soil disturbance to increase organic carbon

entities.name: SOUTHERN AFRICA
entities.description: The geographical region where the farming systems are analyzed

entities.name: SOIL FERTILITY
entities.description: The capacity of soil to provide essential nutrients for plant growth

entities.name: CROP RESIDUES
entities.description: Plant materials remaining after harvest, used for livestock feed or soil mulch

entities.name: LIVESTOCK
entities.description: Farm animals integrated into the cropping system

entities.name: DRY SEASON
entities.description: A climatic condition where water and feed availability are limited

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to sufficient food

entities.name: INCOME
entities.description: Financial returns derived from crop production

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The efficiency of the soil in producing crop yields

entities.name: SUSTAINABILITY
entities.description: The ability of the farming system to maintain productivity over the long term
